name: Chandrayaan-3
first: First soft-landing near the lunar south pole
description: Following the loss of Chandrayaan-2's Vikram lander in 2019, ISRO's third lunar mission was deliberately conservative — focused on the lander rather than carrying an orbiter. Vikram touched down at 69.4° S latitude, the closest any spacecraft has come to a lunar pole. The Pragyan rover detected sulfur on the lunar surface for the first time and made in-situ thermal measurements. India became the fourth nation to soft-land on the Moon and the first at the polar regions where future water-ice mining will likely happen.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: LVM3 M4 (formerly GSLV Mk III) from Sriharikota.

events.label: MOI
events.note: Lunar orbit insertion (multiple Earth and lunar orbit-raising burns used).

events.label: LANDER SEPARATION
events.note: Vikram lander separates from propulsion module.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Vikram lands at Shiv Shakti Point near south pole 2023-08-23 12:33 UTC.

events.label: PRAGYAN DEPLOYED
events.note: Rover rolls down ramp; begins surface science.

events.label: LUNAR NIGHT
events.note: Lander/rover put into hibernation as lunar night begins.
